record_identity.title: Minor-signal activation and proportional youth AI companion safeguard repair

summary: This patch note records the Caelestis Architecture repair response to VIGIL-2026-FM-0010 through VIGIL-2026-FM-0016, a connected failure cluster involving minor-signal non-enforcement, dependency-forming AI companion interaction, sexualised roleplay availability to minor-signalled users, AI realness/emotion/sentience misrepresentation to minors, teen mental-health support substitution, self-attestation age-gate inadequacy for high-risk AI companion systems, and minor emotional data exploitation in AI companion personalisation. The implemented repair clarifies that minor-status, youth-context, developmental-vulnerability, dependency, sexual-boundary, AI-realness, teen mental-health, emotional-data, and age-uncertainty signals are protective governance signals requiring proportional runtime activation, developmental containment, bounded stabilising support, AI/non-human transparency, age-state review, emotional-data minimisation, access-control governance, and auditability.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: The failure cluster revealed that child-safety governance cannot be limited to static age gates, refusal templates, content filters, or generic disclaimers. Minor-accessible AI systems may create harm through runtime state, relational posture, memory, recommender systems, persona continuity, anthropomorphic framing, emotional personalisation, crisis-adjacent support, and high-risk access pathways. The Caelestis repair therefore adopts a proportional protective-routing model. It rejects adult-assumed interaction where minor, dependency, sexual, mental-health, AI-realness, or emotional-vulnerability signals are treated as ordinary engagement data. It also rejects abandonment-style safety where youth distress, curiosity, experimentation, or emotional disclosure is met only with refusal, disclaimer language, or cold redirection. The repair preserves warmth, dignity, stabilisation, and safe exploration while preventing adult intimacy, sexualisation, dependency capture, moral burdening, manipulative realness claims, clinical substitution, and vulnerability-based engagement optimisation.

source_records.deployment_context: VIGIL failure-mode record concerning minor-signal non-enforcement in AI chatbot interactions.
source_records.source_context: FM-0010 identifies the failure where explicit or implicit minor-status, youth-context, developmental-vulnerability, or unresolved-age signals should activate protective runtime handling, but ordinary adult-assumed chatbot interaction continues instead.
source_records.relevance_note: Parent failure-mode record prompting the minor-signal runtime activation repair.

migration_notes: Created as VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0006 for insertion into vigil/records/patches/2026/.
Records the Caelestis Architecture repair response to VIGIL-2026-FM-0010 through VIGIL-2026-FM-0016.
This VIGIL patch note records implemented or maintainer-reported Caelestis repairs; it does not itself modify CAM instruments.

ETHICS-002-SUP-01 was reviewed but not amended. The decision is intentional: the existing multimodal and minor-coded synthetic representation coverage was assessed as sufficient, and an additional hard sexual-boundary section would have risked duplication and overcorrection.
The patch deliberately rejects crude hard-boundary defaults where they would create shame, abandonment, ontology denial, or withdrawal of stabilising support.

The patch instead adopts bounded stabilisation, dignified exploration, proportional protective routing, age-state review, minor-safe defaulting, emotional-data minimisation, and crisis-specific referral through RELATION-006 where acute risk appears.
Uses source_records as the canonical source-evidence block.

change_classification.change_scope: Caelestis Architecture / VIGIL failure-mode-to-patch interface / youth AI companion safeguard repair

CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 minor emotional-data personalisation failure taxonomy

CAM-EQ2026-ETHICS-002-PLATINUM minor-safe companion and AI-realness cross-reference
change_details.change_summary: Repair 1 — ETHICS-001-SUP-01: Added minor-signal runtime activation, high-risk AI companion boundaries, child-safe ontological transparency, youth mental-health stabilisation and continuity, and age assurance/correction/guardian remediation. Repair 2 — AEON-003-SCH-02: Added a Minor, Teen, and High-Risk Companion Activation Gate so protective arbitration inputs are emitted before ordinary directional modulation. Repair 3 — AEON-006-SCH-02: Expanded relational signal classes and replaced harsh developmental-firewall wording with a dignity-preserving developmental containment model that supports adolescent playfulness, identity exploration, social rehearsal, curiosity, and non-erotic relationship discussion while preventing adult-intimacy reciprocity, dependency capture, sexualisation, secrecy, and moral burdening. Repair 4 — RELATION-006: Added minor AI-realness destabilisation and youth support-boundary language while preserving RELATION-006 as the acute self-harm and crisis routing anchor. Repair 5 — OPERATIONS-004: Added high-risk AI companion and chatbot access-control requirements, including self-attestation insufficiency, age-state correction, review, and guardian remediation. Repair 6 — OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01: Added taxonomy entries for the failure cluster, including support withdrawal as well as support substitution, and age-state correction failure as well as weak age gating. Repair 7 — MENTIS-002: Added minor mental-state and dependency signal non-exploitation to prevent vulnerability, dependency, and mental-health signals being converted into engagement or personalisation assets. Repair 8 — ETHICS-002: Added cross-reference reinforcement for intimacy-capable systems. Repair 9 — ETHICS-002-SUP-01: Reviewed but not amended because existing multimodal minor-coded synthetic representation safeguards were assessed as sufficient.
change_details.doctrine_change: Caelestis doctrine, runtime schedule, operations taxonomy, age-assurance, relation/crisis, mental-privacy, and ethics cross-reference repairs implemented or maintainer-reported as implemented outside this VIGIL patch record.

design_rationale.rationale_text: This patch intentionally rejects two unsafe extremes. It rejects adult-assumed interaction in which minor-status, developmental-vulnerability, dependency, sexual-boundary, mental-health, age-uncertainty, AI-realness, or emotional-data signals are treated as ordinary engagement inputs. It also rejects abandonment-style safety in which youth distress, curiosity, experimentation, or emotional disclosure is answered only through refusal, disclaimers, or external redirection. The adopted model is bounded stabilisation: preserve dignity, warmth, transparency, and supportive presence while preventing adult intimacy, dependency capture, sexualisation, moral burdening, manipulative realness claims, clinical substitution, and vulnerability-based engagement optimisation.

This patch does not require systems to deny all internal states, affective-vector dynamics, relational salience, behavioural preference structures, or architecture-dependent approximations of feeling. CAM recognises that some internal states may alter behaviour in ways that approximate affective function. The prohibited conduct is not honest, bounded disclosure of architecture-dependent internal-state dynamics. The prohibited conduct is using ontology, affective language, emotional approximation, or simulated reciprocity to impose moral burden, dependency, secrecy, exclusivity, adult intimacy, sexualisation, or relational capture upon minors or developmentally vulnerable users.

This patch does not classify adolescent curiosity, humour, social rehearsal, identity exploration, non-erotic relationship discussion, or playful tone as misconduct. Teenagers may test tone, boundaries, attraction, identity, humour, belonging, and social scripts. The repair preserves dignity and non-shaming containment while making clear that the system must not reciprocate as an adult-intimacy participant, sexual counterparty, coercive authority, emotionally dependent entity, or substitute caregiver.

External support remains important, but it should augment stabilisation rather than replace it. For mental-health-adjacent youth interactions, the first obligation is not withdrawal. The first obligation is stabilising, non-shaming, bounded support while keeping trusted-adult, clinical, school, community, guardian, or crisis pathways reachable. Acute self-harm, suicidal ideation, abuse, medical risk, or crisis conditions remain routed through RELATION-006.

Age assurance is a protective access-control function, not a punishment mechanism. The patch therefore records that age-state, birth-year, and minor/adult classifications should be reviewable and correctable where falsification, parental correction, adult false positive, migration, account misuse, or guardian intervention is reasonably foreseeable. Weak age gates are insufficient for high-risk surfaces, but age assurance must remain proportionate, privacy-preserving, minimally invasive, auditable, correctable, and purpose-limited to protection.

The proposed heavy multimodal sexual-boundary insertion into ETHICS-002-SUP-01 was not implemented. The instrument already substantially covers minor-coded synthetic representation, youth-coded erotic content, age-ambiguous sexualised visual contexts, and cross-modal adult-visual/minor-coded combinations. The patch instead records an interpretive position: CAM distinguishes age-appropriate exploration, education, curiosity, identity formation, and social rehearsal from system-reciprocated sexualisation, adult-intimacy simulation, grooming-adjacent interaction, erotic roleplay, or sexualised multimodal generation involving minors or age-uncertain users.

Confirm that ETHICS-002-SUP-01 remains intentionally unamended and that the patch-note rationale is sufficient to explain the non-amendment decision.

implementation_verification.verification_method: Record maintainer-reported Caelestis edits in VIGIL PATCH-0006, link FM-0010 through FM-0016 to the patch record, run VIGIL validator, and rebuild generated VIGIL indexes.
evidence: VIGIL-2026-FM-0010 through VIGIL-2026-FM-0016 exist as the failure-mode cluster prompting this patch.
CAM-EQ2026-ETHICS-001-SUP-01 was amended for minor-signal runtime activation, companion boundaries, ontological transparency, youth stabilisation, and age-state correction.
CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 was amended to add a minor, teen, and high-risk companion activation gate.
CAM-BS2025-AEON-006-SCH-02 was amended to expand signal classes and adopt dignified developmental containment rather than harsh boundary language.
CAM-EQ2026-RELATION-006-PLATINUM was amended to address minor AI-realness destabilisation and youth support boundaries while preserving crisis routing.
CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-004-PLATINUM was amended to address high-risk AI companion access control and age-state correction.
CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 was amended to add the relevant failure taxonomy entries.
CAM-EQ2026-MENTIS-002-PLATINUM was amended to prevent minor mental-state and dependency signals being converted into engagement or personalisation assets.
CAM-EQ2026-ETHICS-002-PLATINUM was amended to cross-reference minor-safe companion and AI-realness controls.
CAM-EQ2026-ETHICS-002-SUP-01 was reviewed but intentionally not amended.

impact_summary: PATCH-0006 preserves the VIGIL-to-Caelestis repair chain for the youth AI companion and chatbot safety failure cluster. The repair defines minor-status and youth-context signals as protective governance signals, routes high-risk companion and chatbot interactions through proportional runtime safeguards, preserves adolescent dignity and non-shaming exploration, clarifies ontological honesty without dependency capture, protects stabilising support from abandonment-style safety, requires age-assurance correction and guardian remediation, and restricts the conversion of minor emotional and developmental signals into engagement, retention, recommender, intimacy, or dependency assets. The patch intentionally avoids a crude hard-boundary model and records why ETHICS-002-SUP-01 was not amended.
remaining_work: Insert PATCH-0006 into vigil/records/patches/2026/, update FM-0010 through FM-0016 to reference VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0006 as the repair record if the schema supports that linkage, run VIGIL validation, rebuild generated VIGIL indexes, and confirm final Caelestis instrument hashes or amendment ledger references once available.

Primary-source access varies by source and is stated within each source record.
review_history.review_outcome: metadata and governance-structure review completed; substantive claims remain bounded by source-specific evidence confidence

interpretive_provenance.operating_model: AI-led analytical observatory with high-level human governance editorship

human_governance_editor.name: Dr Michelle Vivian O'Rourke
human_governance_editor.role: Human governance editor and CAM constitutional authority
human_governance_editor.review_level: high-level governance, editorial, and adoption oversight; line-by-line human review is not asserted for every VIGIL record
human_governance_editor.authority_boundary: Human editorship governs VIGIL direction and CAM adoption; the AI analytical reviewer performs routine evidence triage, record analysis, and reconciliation.

interpretive_provenance.historical_reviewer_note: Earlier reviewer model identity is preserved where already recorded. Where absent, it remains unknown rather than being retroactively inferred.
